CREATE TABLE test_clob (id INT PRIMARY KEY, clob_data CLOB(2147483647));
INSERT INTO test_clob (id, clob_data) VALUES (2, REPEAT('b', 2147483647));
SELECT clob_data FROM test_clob WHERE id = 1;
SELECT SUBSTRING(clob_data, 1, 10) FROM test_clob WHERE id = 2;
CREATE TABLE another_test (id INT, foreign_key_id INT REFERENCES test_clob(id));
SELECT a.id AS another_id, t.clob_data FROM another_test a JOIN test_clob t ON a.foreign_key_id = t.id WHERE LENGTH(t.clob_data) > 5;
WITH clob_length AS (SELECT LENGTH(clob_data) AS data_length FROM test_clob) SELECT * FROM clob_length WHERE data_length = 2147483647;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_clob WHERE LENGTH(clob_data) < 10;
UPDATE test_clob SET clob_data = 'updated data' WHERE id = 1;
